name: Mexican Vegetarian Stuffed Peppers
instructions: 1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and position the rack in the center. This moderate temperature will cook the peppers through without burning the cheese topping. 2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat. Add about 1 tablespoon of salt to the water. 3. While waiting for the water to boil, cut the bell peppers in half lengthwise and remove the seeds, membranes, and stems. Try to keep the pepper halves intact. 4. Once the water is boiling, carefully add the bell pepper halves. Cook for 3-4 minutes until they're slightly softened but still hold their shape. They should be tender enough to bite through but not mushy. 5. Using tongs, carefully remove the peppers from the boiling water and drain them well. Arrange the pepper halves cut-side up in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Pat them dry with paper towels if they have excess water inside. 6. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring frequently, until softened and translucent. 7. Transfer the cooked onion to a large mixing bowl. Add the cooked white rice, drained black beans, and diced tomatoes (with their juice). 8. Season the mixture with chili powder, salt, and ground cumin. Stir everything together until well combined. The mixture should be moist but not soupy. 9. Fold in about two-thirds of the Mexican cheese blend (reserve the rest for topping), mixing gently until the cheese is evenly distributed throughout the filling. 10. Using a large spoon, generously fill each pepper half with the rice and bean mixture, mounding it slightly on top. Pack the filling in firmly so it holds together. 11. Sprinkle the remaining Mexican cheese blend evenly over the top of each stuffed pepper, covering the filling completely. 12. Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil (you can spray the underside with cooking spray to prevent cheese from sticking). 13. Bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes. Then remove the foil and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and lightly golden on top. 14. Remove from the oven and let the stuffed peppers rest for 5 minutes before serving. This allows the filling to set slightly and makes them easier to handle. Garnish with fresh cilantro, sour cream, or sliced avocado if desired.
simpleInstructions: 1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). 2. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cut bell peppers in half lengthwise and remove seeds. 3. Boil pepper halves for 3-4 minutes until slightly softened. Drain and arrange cut-side up in a baking dish. 4. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened. 5. Transfer onion to a large bowl. Add rice, black beans, and tomatoes. 6. Stir in chili powder, salt, and cumin. Fold in two-thirds of the cheese blend. 7. Fill each pepper half with the rice mixture, packing it in firmly. 8. Sprinkle remaining cheese over the tops. Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. 9. Remove foil and bake 10-15 more minutes until cheese is golden. Let rest 5 minutes before serving.
